title: dt-pilot backends registry
description: Schema for config/catalog/backends.json - the authoritative registry of supported dt-pilot backends.

id.description: Backend identifier (kebab-case). Must be unique. Used as a dispatch key by tooling and as the canonical name in chat.

displayName.description: Human-readable name shown in tool output.

skill.description: Repo-relative path to the per-backend skill doc.

agentPersona.description: Repo-relative path to the per-backend agent persona, if one exists.

scriptsDir.description: Repo-relative path to the backend's wrappers directory.

catalogFile.description: Repo-relative path to the backend's reflected catalog JSON, if it ships one.

catalogSyncScript.description: Repo-relative path to the catalog-sync script. Must accept '-Check'. Pre-Commit.ps1 iterates this across every backend.

modulesDir.description: Repo-relative path to the directory where catalog-sync writes generated scaffolds.

manifestPattern.description: Repo-relative pattern matching manifest files this backend owns. Pre-Commit.ps1 finds matching files and runs the manifestValidator against each. Supported shapes: '<dir>/**/<filename>' (recursive search for <filename> under <dir>), '<dir>/<filename>' (single-file exact path), or '<filename>' (single-file at repo root). Patterns with '*' outside the recursive '/**/' segment are rejected by the resolver.

manifestValidator.description: Repo-relative path to a script that takes '-Path <manifest>' and exits non-zero on schema failure.

detect.description: Rules used by the Backend Routing section in CLAUDE.md (and by future Sync-McpServerEnablement.ps1) to decide whether this backend is active in a workspace. Detection is informational today; tooling will consume it as backends accumulate.

path.description: For 'file-exists': repo-relative path to check. For 'file-exists-recursive': starting path for a recursive search.

extension.description: For 'file-extension-present': extension to look for under the repo (e.g. '.tf').

match.description: Optional content-shape token the detector should verify (e.g. 'monaco-manifest' means the matched file must contain a 'manifestVersion:' line). Reserved for future use; consumers should ignore unknown match tokens.
